category_title: High-achieving dyslexic students who use deep-level strategies (organization, elaboration, monitoring) to compensate for decoding deficits
scenario_title: Expository text study and note-taking under a deadline
minimal_context: The student is reading an 891-word social studies text, copying notes from PowerPoint slides, and answering textbook questions before a class deadline.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We have a deadline coming up. Please read this 891-word social studies text by yourself, copy the notes from the PowerPoint slides, and then answer the textbook questions.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I will try to answer the questions first, and if I cannot answer them, I will look back in the text. I have to economize on my resources, so I am only going to make notes on the most important information.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Okay, just keep working on it and make sure you read the text.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I feel that I don’t learn very well from merely reading a text by myself. If I am not interested in it, then it will take incredibly long to learn it.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Try your best to get it done before the time is up.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I am getting really stressed working toward this deadline. I am being slowed by having to cope with the difficult words in the text, and it takes too long for me to write down what you presented on the slides.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We have a deadline coming up. Please read this 891-word social studies text by yourself, copy the notes from the PowerPoint slides, and then answer the textbook questions.

STUDENT: I will try to answer the questions first, and if I cannot answer them, I will look back in the text. I have to economize on my resources, so I am only going to make notes on the most important information.

TUTOR: Okay, just keep working on it and make sure you read the text.

STUDENT: I feel that I don’t learn very well from merely reading a text by myself. If I am not interested in it, then it will take incredibly long to learn it.

TUTOR: Try your best to get it done before the time is up.

STUDENT: I am getting really stressed working toward this deadline. I am being slowed by having to cope with the difficult words in the text, and it takes too long for me to write down what you presented on the slides.

task.content: Studying an 891-word expository social studies text and answering textbook questions
task.learning_goal: Assessing their own understanding of text content

context.environment: Classroom or tutoring setting working toward deadlines
context.social_setting: Instruction with a teacher/tutor followed by independent or collaborative processing

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When having to cope with difficult words in the text and trying to write down what the teacher presented during lessons
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Slowed by having to cope with difficult words; taking too long to write down what the teachers presented during lessons
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Stressed when working toward deadlines; feeling that learning takes incredibly long if not interested
related_quotes: I feel that I don’t learn very well from merely reading a text by myself.
If I am not interested in it, then it will take incredibly long to learn it.

behavior_patterns.condition: When assessing their own understanding of text content using textbook questions
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Tries to answer the questions, and if they cannot answer them, looks back in the text

behavior_patterns.condition: When dealing with large amounts of learning materials
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Economizes on their resources by making and using notes on the most important information
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Acknowledging that they had to economize on their resources because of the extra time and effort that they had to invest in learning
